$400 at Amazon $426 at Newegg

This ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi motherboard is one to buy if you fancy building a small form factor PC. It's seriously small, rocking just one PCI slot. This size does mean you will lose some features, including only having one PCI slot, but you're able to create something special with a smaller footprint. ASUS managed to include a 10+2 phase power delivery design and the flagship X670E chipset. This means you should be able to run even the AMD Ryzen 9 without issues, though overclocking may produce some unstable results with the higher-tier processors. The single PCIe slot we mentioned earlier is 5.0, so too is one of two available PCIe M.2 slots for SSDs.

There's not a lot to the board itself, due to the size, but ASUS did a fine job in making it aesthetically pleasing and stealthy enough to fit inside more themed builds. There are a few headers for cooling and RGB lighting integration, as well as all the usual power connections. An added bonus of the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi is the inclusion of the ROG Strix Hive. This external device hooks up to the motherboard and offers additional functionality, including an integrated DAC. This can prove useful if you position your compact PC somewhere that makes the rear I/O difficult to access.

For rear I/O, Asus managed to squeeze in two USB-C 4.0 ports, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, and a further three USB-A 2.0 ports, so even the most compact PC system will be able to handle a few gaming peripherals.

MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
Best for enthusiasts

Try your luck at the overclocking leaderboard!

$1200 $1262 Save $62

The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is an extreme motherboard with just about everything you could ask for in an enthusiastic platform. Designed for heavier use, MSI offers multiple reinforced PCIe 5.0 slots, four M.2 slots, robust power delivery with effective VRM cooling, and a striking dark board design.

$1200 at Amazon $1243 at Newegg

You likely won't require anything close to the MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE, but it's designed for those with more advanced hardware and plans for some overclocking. Let's start with the impressive power setup, consisting of a 24+2+1 phase delivery design. The VRMs are covered by a large heatsink and MSI made good use of some thermal pads and MOSFET passive cooling. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 codec with help from the ESS ES9280AQ, which means movies, music, and (most importantly) games will sound amazing when paired with the best gaming headset.

For RAM, MSI states the board will happily support DDR5 modules with speeds up to 5600MT/s. The DIMM slots are conveniently located next to the migrated 4-pin EPS sockets. In fact, just about every major connection header on this motherboard has been adjusted to face perpendicular to the motherboard, instead of outward. This should aid in cable routing, especially for the thicker cabling. All three PCI slots are reinforced and support PCIe 5.0 for maximum bandwidth. If all that wasn't enough, there are four M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0), and 10GbE networking.

The rear I/O is just as ridiculous with seven USB-A 3.2 Gen2 ports, and two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 ports. The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is incredibly expensive, almost triple the price of some of our recommendations here, but the price may be worth it if you require all these advanced features not typically found on lesser boards.

ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best workstation

For serious computing and performance

$880 $900 Save $20

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

$900 at Newegg $880 at Amazon

Last, but not least, we've added the ASRock WRX80 Creator as our pick for the best motherboard for those looking to build a HEDT PC with an AMD Ryzen Threadripper processor. The ASRock WRX80 Creator is one of the most expensive motherboards you can buy, but that's because it comes with a long list of features that are not necessarily found on any of the mainstream motherboards we've listed above. It serves up to 64 lanes of PCIe at Gen4 speeds. It's also only the motherboard in this list that comes with a dual-fan heatsink covering its 8 105A voltage regulator phases.

The ASRock WRX80 Creator is an ATX motherboard that carries an AMD sWRX socket. Two 40mm fans are cooling the VRM, in addition to the regular 50mm fan that we usually see on other mainstream AMD motherboards. These VRM fans are enough to tame even the most powerful, fully-overclocked system without a hitch. There are two available M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots for SSDs, joining the seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots for expansion cards.

The I/O shield comes pre-installed, which isn't all that surprising considering this is a high-end premium motherboard. You won't see any RGB lighting, however. The ASRock WRX80 Creator comes with 8 DIMM slots for impressive system memory capacity. You get plenty of headers for fan/pump, RGB lights, USB, and more. Overclocking is also in cards since this is a high-performance motherboard that's designed to push even the most demanding HEDT CPUs on the market.

Choosing the best AMD motherboard

It's not required to spend a fortune to enjoy the best of what's offered by AMD desktop CPUs, and we believe our collection here provides ample choice, covering multiple generations. We view the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming as the best X670 chipset-based motherboard one can buy right now for a high-end PC build. It has a solid power phase delivery system for stable CPU overclocking, plenty of connectivity, and an excellent set of features ... for a price.

The ASUS TUF Gaming B650-PLUS is also a great option for those looking to buy an affordable AM5 motherboard. It has many of the same features offered by the X670E-E Gaming from ASUS but cuts a few to bring the price down. If you do not need the absolute best platform for enthusiast overclocking and likely won't saturate all the available PCIe lanes offered by the B650 chipset, this motherboard will be more than enough.

Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall
$460 $500 Save $40

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$460 at Amazon $460 at Newegg $460 at B&H
item.description:
Picking out the best motherboard for AMD CPUs requires a compatible socket and chipset for the processor in question. Newer AMD processors support motherboards with an AM5 chipset, and thus we'll be focusing on these options. It's possible to buy a decent motherboard for AMD chips for as little as $100 or as much as $1,000, depending on what features are important to your system build. All the motherboards in our buyer guide will work well with the best CPUs from AMD.

1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, 10x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 ports
Pros
  • Excellent performance
  • Five M.2 SSD slots
  • Good overclocking support
Cons
  • Pricey
  • No 5Gb LAN
$460 at Amazon $460 at Newegg $460 at B&H

One of our favorite motherboards for AMD Ryzen 7000 processors is the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming. It has almost everything you need for a stable PC build, even with the flagship AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Let's start with the X670E chipset. It's an "extreme" version of the X670, reserved for more enthusiast-grade boards due to it being better for overclocking and having all the connectivity in the world. We've got an 18+2 phase power delivery design for the CPU and other nearby components, as well as two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots, one PCIe 4.0 x16 slot, three PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD slots, and a single PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSD slot.

Source: XDA-Developers

This motherboard can work with the latest GPUs and storage, as well as many future products down the line. We're not using anywhere close to the limit of PCIe 5.0 for graphics cards, making the ASUS ROG Strix X670-E Gaming a good choice for the future. For USB ports on the rear I/O, ASUS made sure you have plenty of options, including 12 USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and a further three more USB 2.0 ports. This is all rounded out with 2.5Gb networking for speedy access to your LAN and the outside world. It's simply one of the better X670E motherboards without spending a ridiculous amount of money.

GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
Runner-up

Another great AM5 motherboard choice

$249 $290 Save $41

The Gigabyte X670 Aorus Elite AX has the essentials for a high-end gaming PC, including support for DDR5-6666 memory, four M.2 slots for SSDs (one of which supports PCIe 5.0), and a 16-stage VRM.

Memory Type
DDR5-6666+
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases

Networking

Internal I/O

Rear I/O

Pros
  • Great performance
  • Good overclock potential
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No PCIe 5.0 slot
$249 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at Best Buy

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is another top recommendation from us for AMD Ryzen 7000+ processors and the AM5 socket. Just like the Asus X670 motherboard we recommended above, this AORUS Elite AX board has plenty of premium features, including speedy networking, rapid M.2 storage slots, and decent power delivery for some overclocking. Beefy heatsinks cover the VRMs and with a 16+2+2 phase power delivery design, you shouldn't encounter any issues regarding heat. The rear I/O has numerous connection points, including 2.5GbE networking, six USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and four USB-A 2.0 ports.

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX motherboard has two 8-pin connectors to feed power to the CPU. You also get a ton of fan/pump, USB, and RGB headers on this motherboard. Four RAM DIMM slots are present and can handle up to 128GB at DDR5-6666+ speeds. Your mileage is bound to vary regarding speeds, since not all memory modules have the same speeds.

The motherboard also comes with three PCIe slots. Two of those are full-length reinforced slots, whereas the last one is an x1 slot at the bottom without any reinforcement. We also like the fact that the I/O shield is pre-installed on this motherboard, which means that's one less step to worry about while building a PC.

Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
Best value

Save some money and still build a powerful PC

$210 $215 Save $5

The Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus is a midrange motherboard with support for PCIe 5.0 SSDs and midrange to high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases

PCI Slots
2x PCIe 4.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x1

Networking

Internal I/O

Rear I/O

Pros
  • Great value
  • DDR5 and PCIe 5.0 support
Cons
  • Only three PCIe M.2 slots
  • Limited rear I/O
$210 at Amazon $210 at Newegg $210 at Best Buy

Going with the latest AMD Ryzen processors doesn't mean you need to spend a fortune, even on a motherboard with an AM5 socket. The Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS rocks the AMD B650 chipset, which only loses a few features compared to the flagship X670 chipset, and we've seen motherboard vendors work some serious magic with this thing. We've got a 12+2 phase power delivery design, which should allow you to perform a little overclocking, as well as a three-year limited warranty should anything happen to go wrong. The full-size ATX form factor allowed Asus to cram as many PCIe slots as possible and this board has two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, followed by two additional PCIe 4.0 x1 slots.

For storage, you'll be able to utilize the array of SATA ports and two PCIe M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0, the other 4.0). Plenty of USB ports can be found on the rear, as well as the 2.5Gb networking, which is great for upgrading your LAN further down the line. Four DIMM slots support a maximum of 128GB of DDR5 RAM, which is capable of hitting speeds up to 6400 MT/s. Even though we're looking at a lower-tier chipset and no PCIe 5.0 slots for GPUs and other expansion cards, Asus made sure to spend the necessary budget on some of the essentials.

The standard ATX form factor allowed Asus to space everything out for a cleaner aesthetic. There isn't a massive shroud covering most of the PCB and, with everything being black, it's a stealthy-looking motherboard. It's not the most striking board in terms of design, but we've come to appreciate ASUS' more industrial look of its TUF products. If you're looking for one of the best-value AM5 motherboards for an AMD Ryzen 7000 PC build, consider giving the Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS a go.

ASRock B650M-HDV-m2
Best budget
$110 $140 Save $30

The ASRock B650M-HDV/M.2 is the motherboard to buy for more budget-friendly AMD-powered PC builds. If you want to spend less than $130, this motherboard has everything you need for AMD's latest chips.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
2

Rear I/O

$138 at Newegg $110 at Amazon

The ASRock B650M HDV/M.2 is the most affordable motherboard in our collection here. It uses the budget-friendly B650M chipset from AMD, which supports DDR5 RAM, the latest AMD Ryzen processors, and is capable of handling even an AMD Ryzen 9 7590X, so long as no overclocking profiles are configured. It's a basic platform for a PC build with the bare essentials all present. You won't find more advanced features such as PCIe 5.0 support on the expansion slots, but for the price, we're not complaining.

Rocking an 8+2+1 phase power delivery design, the B650M HDV/M.2 is great for every Ryzen 7000 series processor. There's even PCIe 5.0 support for M.2 storage, which is incredible at this price point. Then there's the 2.5GbE networking, something which we still see absent on other (sometimes more expensive) motherboards. The board itself has an aesthetically pleasing design with some heatsinks for the VRMs and B650M chipset. USB connectivity on the rear is weak but ASRock makes up for this with front-panel USB 3.0 and USB-C support.

It won't win any awards for performance or features, but the ASRock B650M HDV/M.2 gets far more right than wrong, making it a solid choice for even a budget-friendly AMD gaming PC.

ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi
Best last-gen AM4

Keep your old Ryzen CPU alive with this budget board

$170 $190 Save $20

The Asus ROG Strix B550-F Gaming is one of the better B550 boards on the market, and it's a great option for those looking to build a powerful AMD-based build with one of the 5000-series processors.

Form Factor

Networking

Rear I/O
4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 6x USB 2.0
Pros
  • Good value
  • Great performance
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No DDR5 or PCIe 5.0 support
$170 at Amazon $190 at B&H $190 at Newegg

The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is one of the best B550 motherboards you can buy for your AMD CPUs. It offers most of the features and capabilities of more expensive X570-chipset-based boards with an affordable price tag. It may not be the best motherboard for your AMD CPU, but it is what offers the most value for your money and is perfect for a budget build. The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi features a 14-phase delivery, 2.5GB LAN, integrated Wi-Fi 6, two M.2 slots, and more. The motherboard VRM is covered with a heatsink, and it comes with two EPS connectors.

You also get a ton of fan/pump headers, in addition to a bunch of RGB and USB headers on the motherboard. DDR4 support is present for up to 5100 MT/s for system memory, allowing you to match this board with a high-power AMD Ryzen 5000-series processor. Two full-sized PCIe slots are available with the primary one being reinforced for accepting GPUs. It provides 16 PCIe 4.0 lanes to the GPU, whereas the second slot runs at PCIe 3.0 x4 speeds. In terms of connectivity, you get a total of eight USB ports, which include two USB 3.2 Gen 2, four USB 3.1 Gen1, and two USB 2.0. One of the USB 2.0 ports is designated for BIOS flash functionality.

In addition to the USB ports, you also get a single Intel 2.5 GbE LAN port, an HDMI, and DisplayPort, along with an audio stack consisting of 5-plug analog outputs plus SPDIF. The performance of the ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is also on-point. It supports even the most demanding AMD CPU on the market. That being said, it's best to pair this particular motherboard with, say, an AMD Ryzen 5 5600X CPU for a mid-range build instead of a high-end rig. As such, it shouldn't have any issues running any 3000 or 5000 series CPU thanks to the AM4 socket and the B550 chipset. This motherboard is readily available on the market right now, but be sure to check out this link below to find the best price online.

NZXT N7 B650E Gaming Wi-Fi 6 Motherboard
Best design

This is the board to buy for a showcase PC

$280 $316 Save $36

stylish metal-clad motherboard for clean white builds.

Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, 2x USB-A 2.0 ports, 1x HDMI 2.1, 5x Audio jacks, S/PDIF out
Pros
  • Gorgeous design
  • Great B650 performance
  • Plenty of connectivity
Cons
  • Shroud may block some 24-pin ATX connectors
$280 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at NZXT

NZXT made a splash in the motherboard space with its N7 Z490 board for Intel builds, following which it quickly became a popular choice for a lot of people. The company quickly followed up with an AMD version, and now we've got a board built for AMD's AM5 platform. The NZXT N7 B650E is our pick for the best motherboard design. It's stunning with a matte black or white shroud. The N7 B650E comes with PCIe 5.0 support, Wi-Fi 6E, four SATA ports, and a ton of USB ports for connecting all kinds of peripherals. It's one of the best-looking motherboards on this list, which looks best when paired with an NZXT PC case.

The NZXT N7 B650E motherboard comes in a black-and-white color scheme. It comes with a rather sophisticated heatsink/shroud covering almost the entirety of the motherboard. The only visible part of the PCB is next to the AM5 CPU socket, which will eventually get covered by the CPU heatsink. The heavily-shrouded design isn't for everyone, but it'll nicely blend into any and all kinds of PC builds without any issues. Those who don't like RGB lights on their motherboard will particularly like the N7 B550 since it doesn't have any RGB lights whatsoever. So if you're into RGB lighting, you'll have to look elsewhere.

The NZXT N7 B650E comes with two EPS connectors and a bunch of headers for your fan/pump, RGB, and USB ports. You also get two 4-pin NZXT RGB headers to control compatible NZXT products. You also get four DRAM slots on this board, although none of them are reinforced. It supports up to 128GB of RAM with listed speeds of up to DDR5-6000 (OC). The board has three PCIe slots in total, out of which only the top slot is reinforced for the GPU. You also get three M.2 slots that accept drives up to 80mm long. NZXT attempted to get the most out of the B650 chipset, rivaling feature sets of more expensive boards.

Just like other premium motherboards in this collection, the NZXT N7 B650E also comes with a pre-installed I/O shield. You get ten USB ports in total, out of which four are USB 3.2 Gen2 ports, four are USB 3.2 Gen1 ports, and the final two are USB 2.0 ports. You also get an HDMI port along with BIOS flashback and clear CMOS buttons and a Wi-Fi antenna. The performance of the N7 B650E is on par with a lot of other premium motherboards on the market. It's a fantastic option for those looking at a clean motherboard that hides the PCB design. It's particularly good for builds involving NZXT PC cases or other NZXT components.

ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI
Best compact

Build a super-compact gaming PC with this board

$400 $470 Save $70

This compact ITX ASUS ROG Strix X670E motherboard has everything you'll need for a powerful PC that can fit inside a smaller form factor chassis.

Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
10+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
2x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
ALC4050
Networking
Intel 2.5Gb, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB 3.2-C Gen 2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1
Rear I/O

Pros
  • Compact design
  • Good performance
Cons
  • Limited connectivity
  • Expensive
$400 at Amazon $426 at Newegg

This ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi motherboard is one to buy if you fancy building a small form factor PC. It's seriously small, rocking just one PCI slot. This size does mean you will lose some features, including only having one PCI slot, but you're able to create something special with a smaller footprint. ASUS managed to include a 10+2 phase power delivery design and the flagship X670E chipset. This means you should be able to run even the AMD Ryzen 9 without issues, though overclocking may produce some unstable results with the higher-tier processors. The single PCIe slot we mentioned earlier is 5.0, so too is one of two available PCIe M.2 slots for SSDs.

There's not a lot to the board itself, due to the size, but ASUS did a fine job in making it aesthetically pleasing and stealthy enough to fit inside more themed builds. There are a few headers for cooling and RGB lighting integration, as well as all the usual power connections. An added bonus of the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi is the inclusion of the ROG Strix Hive. This external device hooks up to the motherboard and offers additional functionality, including an integrated DAC. This can prove useful if you position your compact PC somewhere that makes the rear I/O difficult to access.

For rear I/O, Asus managed to squeeze in two USB-C 4.0 ports, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, and a further three USB-A 2.0 ports, so even the most compact PC system will be able to handle a few gaming peripherals.

MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
Best for enthusiasts

Try your luck at the overclocking leaderboard!

$1200 $1262 Save $62

The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is an extreme motherboard with just about everything you could ask for in an enthusiastic platform. Designed for heavier use, MSI offers multiple reinforced PCIe 5.0 slots, four M.2 slots, robust power delivery with effective VRM cooling, and a striking dark board design.

Brand
MSI
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-5600
Form Factor
E-ATX
CPU Support
AMD Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
24+2+1
PCI Slots
3x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
4x M.2, 8x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC4082 Codec, ESS ES9280AQ
Networking
10GbE, 2.5GbE, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 4x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 7x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2
Pros
  • Impressive specifications
  • Excellent performance
  • Board design
Cons
  • Incredibly expensive
  • Overkill for most systems
$1200 at Amazon $1243 at Newegg

You likely won't require anything close to the MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE, but it's designed for those with more advanced hardware and plans for some overclocking. Let's start with the impressive power setup, consisting of a 24+2+1 phase delivery design. The VRMs are covered by a large heatsink and MSI made good use of some thermal pads and MOSFET passive cooling. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 codec with help from the ESS ES9280AQ, which means movies, music, and (most importantly) games will sound amazing when paired with the best gaming headset.

For RAM, MSI states the board will happily support DDR5 modules with speeds up to 5600MT/s. The DIMM slots are conveniently located next to the migrated 4-pin EPS sockets. In fact, just about every major connection header on this motherboard has been adjusted to face perpendicular to the motherboard, instead of outward. This should aid in cable routing, especially for the thicker cabling. All three PCI slots are reinforced and support PCIe 5.0 for maximum bandwidth. If all that wasn't enough, there are four M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0), and 10GbE networking.

The rear I/O is just as ridiculous with seven USB-A 3.2 Gen2 ports, and two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 ports. The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is incredibly expensive, almost triple the price of some of our recommendations here, but the price may be worth it if you require all these advanced features not typically found on lesser boards.

ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best workstation

For serious computing and performance

$880 $900 Save $20

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

Memory Slots
8
Memory Type
DDR4-4400
Form Factor
E-ATX
Wi-Fi
Yes
CPU Support
AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 3000/5000
Chipset
WRX80
Socket
sWRX
Power Phases
8
PCI Slots
7x PCIe 4.0
Storage
8x SATA, 2x M.2 SSD
Audio
Realtek ALC4050H+ALC1220
Networking
2x Marvell (Aquantia) 10Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB 2.0
Rear I/O
2x Thunderbolt 4, 4x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen1
Pros
  • Amazing performance
  • Incredible bandwidth
  • Dual 10GbE networking
Cons
  • Expensive
  • Supports only Threadripper CPUs
$900 at Newegg $880 at Amazon

Last, but not least, we've added the ASRock WRX80 Creator as our pick for the best motherboard for those looking to build a HEDT PC with an AMD Ryzen Threadripper processor. The ASRock WRX80 Creator is one of the most expensive motherboards you can buy, but that's because it comes with a long list of features that are not necessarily found on any of the mainstream motherboards we've listed above. It serves up to 64 lanes of PCIe at Gen4 speeds. It's also only the motherboard in this list that comes with a dual-fan heatsink covering its 8 105A voltage regulator phases.

The ASRock WRX80 Creator is an ATX motherboard that carries an AMD sWRX socket. Two 40mm fans are cooling the VRM, in addition to the regular 50mm fan that we usually see on other mainstream AMD motherboards. These VRM fans are enough to tame even the most powerful, fully-overclocked system without a hitch. There are two available M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots for SSDs, joining the seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots for expansion cards.

The I/O shield comes pre-installed, which isn't all that surprising considering this is a high-end premium motherboard. You won't see any RGB lighting, however. The ASRock WRX80 Creator comes with 8 DIMM slots for impressive system memory capacity. You get plenty of headers for fan/pump, RGB lights, USB, and more. Overclocking is also in cards since this is a high-performance motherboard that's designed to push even the most demanding HEDT CPUs on the market.

Choosing the best AMD motherboard

It's not required to spend a fortune to enjoy the best of what's offered by AMD desktop CPUs, and we believe our collection here provides ample choice, covering multiple generations. We view the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming as the best X670 chipset-based motherboard one can buy right now for a high-end PC build. It has a solid power phase delivery system for stable CPU overclocking, plenty of connectivity, and an excellent set of features ... for a price.

The ASUS TUF Gaming B650-PLUS is also a great option for those looking to buy an affordable AM5 motherboard. It has many of the same features offered by the X670E-E Gaming from ASUS but cuts a few to bring the price down. If you do not need the absolute best platform for enthusiast overclocking and likely won't saturate all the available PCIe lanes offered by the B650 chipset, this motherboard will be more than enough.

Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall
$460 $500 Save $40

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$460 at Amazon $460 at Newegg $460 at B&H
item.content:encoded:
Picking out the best motherboard for AMD CPUs requires a compatible socket and chipset for the processor in question. Newer AMD processors support motherboards with an AM5 chipset, and thus we'll be focusing on these options. It's possible to buy a decent motherboard for AMD chips for as little as $100 or as much as $1,000, depending on what features are important to your system build. All the motherboards in our buyer guide will work well with the best CPUs from AMD.

One of our favorite motherboards for AMD Ryzen 7000 processors is the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming. It has almost everything you need for a stable PC build, even with the flagship AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Let's start with the X670E chipset. It's an "extreme" version of the X670, reserved for more enthusiast-grade boards due to it being better for overclocking and having all the connectivity in the world. We've got an 18+2 phase power delivery design for the CPU and other nearby components, as well as two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots, one PCIe 4.0 x16 slot, three PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD slots, and a single PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSD slot.

Source: XDA-Developers

This motherboard can work with the latest GPUs and storage, as well as many future products down the line. We're not using anywhere close to the limit of PCIe 5.0 for graphics cards, making the ASUS ROG Strix X670-E Gaming a good choice for the future. For USB ports on the rear I/O, ASUS made sure you have plenty of options, including 12 USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and a further three more USB 2.0 ports. This is all rounded out with 2.5Gb networking for speedy access to your LAN and the outside world. It's simply one of the better X670E motherboards without spending a ridiculous amount of money.

GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
Runner-up

Another great AM5 motherboard choice

$249 $290 Save $41

The Gigabyte X670 Aorus Elite AX has the essentials for a high-end gaming PC, including support for DDR5-6666 memory, four M.2 slots for SSDs (one of which supports PCIe 5.0), and a 16-stage VRM.

Memory Type
DDR5-6666+
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 1x PCIe 4.0 x4, 1x PCIe 3.0 x1
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 4x SATA
Networking
2.5 GbE, AMD Wi-Fi 6E RZ616
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB 2.0
Rear I/O
6x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 4x USB-A 2.0
Pros
  • Great performance
  • Good overclock potential
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No PCIe 5.0 slot
$249 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at Best Buy

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is another top recommendation from us for AMD Ryzen 7000+ processors and the AM5 socket. Just like the Asus X670 motherboard we recommended above, this AORUS Elite AX board has plenty of premium features, including speedy networking, rapid M.2 storage slots, and decent power delivery for some overclocking. Beefy heatsinks cover the VRMs and with a 16+2+2 phase power delivery design, you shouldn't encounter any issues regarding heat. The rear I/O has numerous connection points, including 2.5GbE networking, six USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and four USB-A 2.0 ports.

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX motherboard has two 8-pin connectors to feed power to the CPU. You also get a ton of fan/pump, USB, and RGB headers on this motherboard. Four RAM DIMM slots are present and can handle up to 128GB at DDR5-6666+ speeds. Your mileage is bound to vary regarding speeds, since not all memory modules have the same speeds.

The motherboard also comes with three PCIe slots. Two of those are full-length reinforced slots, whereas the last one is an x1 slot at the bottom without any reinforcement. We also like the fact that the I/O shield is pre-installed on this motherboard, which means that's one less step to worry about while building a PC.

Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
Best value

Save some money and still build a powerful PC

$210 $215 Save $5

The Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus is a midrange motherboard with support for PCIe 5.0 SSDs and midrange to high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
12+2
PCI Slots
2x PCIe 4.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x1
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Networking
1x Realtek 2.5Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB 3.2 Gen 2, 4x USB 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB 2.0
Pros
  • Great value
  • DDR5 and PCIe 5.0 support
Cons
  • Only three PCIe M.2 slots
  • Limited rear I/O
$210 at Amazon $210 at Newegg $210 at Best Buy

Going with the latest AMD Ryzen processors doesn't mean you need to spend a fortune, even on a motherboard with an AM5 socket. The Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS rocks the AMD B650 chipset, which only loses a few features compared to the flagship X670 chipset, and we've seen motherboard vendors work some serious magic with this thing. We've got a 12+2 phase power delivery design, which should allow you to perform a little overclocking, as well as a three-year limited warranty should anything happen to go wrong. The full-size ATX form factor allowed Asus to cram as many PCIe slots as possible and this board has two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, followed by two additional PCIe 4.0 x1 slots.

For storage, you'll be able to utilize the array of SATA ports and two PCIe M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0, the other 4.0). Plenty of USB ports can be found on the rear, as well as the 2.5Gb networking, which is great for upgrading your LAN further down the line. Four DIMM slots support a maximum of 128GB of DDR5 RAM, which is capable of hitting speeds up to 6400 MT/s. Even though we're looking at a lower-tier chipset and no PCIe 5.0 slots for GPUs and other expansion cards, Asus made sure to spend the necessary budget on some of the essentials.

The standard ATX form factor allowed Asus to space everything out for a cleaner aesthetic. There isn't a massive shroud covering most of the PCB and, with everything being black, it's a stealthy-looking motherboard. It's not the most striking board in terms of design, but we've come to appreciate ASUS' more industrial look of its TUF products. If you're looking for one of the best-value AM5 motherboards for an AMD Ryzen 7000 PC build, consider giving the Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS a go.

ASRock B650M-HDV-m2
Best budget
$110 $140 Save $30

The ASRock B650M-HDV/M.2 is the motherboard to buy for more budget-friendly AMD-powered PC builds. If you want to spend less than $130, this motherboard has everything you need for AMD's latest chips.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
2
Memory Type
DDR5-6400+
Form Factor
M-ATX
CPU Support
Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
8+2+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 1x PCIe 4.0 x4, 1x PCIe 4.0 x1
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC897
Networking
Realtek 2.5Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 2.0, 1x Thunderbolt header
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1 port, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 4x USB-A 2.0 ports
$138 at Newegg $110 at Amazon

The ASRock B650M HDV/M.2 is the most affordable motherboard in our collection here. It uses the budget-friendly B650M chipset from AMD, which supports DDR5 RAM, the latest AMD Ryzen processors, and is capable of handling even an AMD Ryzen 9 7590X, so long as no overclocking profiles are configured. It's a basic platform for a PC build with the bare essentials all present. You won't find more advanced features such as PCIe 5.0 support on the expansion slots, but for the price, we're not complaining.

Rocking an 8+2+1 phase power delivery design, the B650M HDV/M.2 is great for every Ryzen 7000 series processor. There's even PCIe 5.0 support for M.2 storage, which is incredible at this price point. Then there's the 2.5GbE networking, something which we still see absent on other (sometimes more expensive) motherboards. The board itself has an aesthetically pleasing design with some heatsinks for the VRMs and B650M chipset. USB connectivity on the rear is weak but ASRock makes up for this with front-panel USB 3.0 and USB-C support.

It won't win any awards for performance or features, but the ASRock B650M HDV/M.2 gets far more right than wrong, making it a solid choice for even a budget-friendly AMD gaming PC.

ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi
Best last-gen AM4

Keep your old Ryzen CPU alive with this budget board

$170 $190 Save $20

The Asus ROG Strix B550-F Gaming is one of the better B550 boards on the market, and it's a great option for those looking to build a powerful AMD-based build with one of the 5000-series processors.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B550
Socket
AM4
Power Phases
12+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 1x PCIe 3.0 x16, 3x PCIe 3.0 x1
Storage
6x SATA, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 3.0 M.2
Audio
S1220A
Networking
Intel I225-V 2.5Gb, Intel Wi-Fi 6 AX200
Internal I/O
4 x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 6x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 6x USB 2.0
Pros
  • Good value
  • Great performance
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No DDR5 or PCIe 5.0 support
$170 at Amazon $190 at B&H $190 at Newegg

The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is one of the best B550 motherboards you can buy for your AMD CPUs. It offers most of the features and capabilities of more expensive X570-chipset-based boards with an affordable price tag. It may not be the best motherboard for your AMD CPU, but it is what offers the most value for your money and is perfect for a budget build. The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi features a 14-phase delivery, 2.5GB LAN, integrated Wi-Fi 6, two M.2 slots, and more. The motherboard VRM is covered with a heatsink, and it comes with two EPS connectors.

You also get a ton of fan/pump headers, in addition to a bunch of RGB and USB headers on the motherboard. DDR4 support is present for up to 5100 MT/s for system memory, allowing you to match this board with a high-power AMD Ryzen 5000-series processor. Two full-sized PCIe slots are available with the primary one being reinforced for accepting GPUs. It provides 16 PCIe 4.0 lanes to the GPU, whereas the second slot runs at PCIe 3.0 x4 speeds. In terms of connectivity, you get a total of eight USB ports, which include two USB 3.2 Gen 2, four USB 3.1 Gen1, and two USB 2.0. One of the USB 2.0 ports is designated for BIOS flash functionality.

In addition to the USB ports, you also get a single Intel 2.5 GbE LAN port, an HDMI, and DisplayPort, along with an audio stack consisting of 5-plug analog outputs plus SPDIF. The performance of the ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is also on-point. It supports even the most demanding AMD CPU on the market. That being said, it's best to pair this particular motherboard with, say, an AMD Ryzen 5 5600X CPU for a mid-range build instead of a high-end rig. As such, it shouldn't have any issues running any 3000 or 5000 series CPU thanks to the AM4 socket and the B550 chipset. This motherboard is readily available on the market right now, but be sure to check out this link below to find the best price online.

NZXT N7 B650E Gaming Wi-Fi 6 Motherboard
Best design

This is the board to buy for a showcase PC

$280 $316 Save $36

stylish metal-clad motherboard for clean white builds.

Brand
NZXT
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-6000+
Form Factor
ATX
CPU Support
Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x16
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC1220
Networking
Realtek 2.5Gb, 2x2 Wi-Fi 6E (802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax)
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 3x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, 2x USB-A 2.0 ports, 1x HDMI 2.1, 5x Audio jacks, S/PDIF out
Pros
  • Gorgeous design
  • Great B650 performance
  • Plenty of connectivity
Cons
  • Shroud may block some 24-pin ATX connectors
$280 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at NZXT

NZXT made a splash in the motherboard space with its N7 Z490 board for Intel builds, following which it quickly became a popular choice for a lot of people. The company quickly followed up with an AMD version, and now we've got a board built for AMD's AM5 platform. The NZXT N7 B650E is our pick for the best motherboard design. It's stunning with a matte black or white shroud. The N7 B650E comes with PCIe 5.0 support, Wi-Fi 6E, four SATA ports, and a ton of USB ports for connecting all kinds of peripherals. It's one of the best-looking motherboards on this list, which looks best when paired with an NZXT PC case.

The NZXT N7 B650E motherboard comes in a black-and-white color scheme. It comes with a rather sophisticated heatsink/shroud covering almost the entirety of the motherboard. The only visible part of the PCB is next to the AM5 CPU socket, which will eventually get covered by the CPU heatsink. The heavily-shrouded design isn't for everyone, but it'll nicely blend into any and all kinds of PC builds without any issues. Those who don't like RGB lights on their motherboard will particularly like the N7 B550 since it doesn't have any RGB lights whatsoever. So if you're into RGB lighting, you'll have to look elsewhere.

The NZXT N7 B650E comes with two EPS connectors and a bunch of headers for your fan/pump, RGB, and USB ports. You also get two 4-pin NZXT RGB headers to control compatible NZXT products. You also get four DRAM slots on this board, although none of them are reinforced. It supports up to 128GB of RAM with listed speeds of up to DDR5-6000 (OC). The board has three PCIe slots in total, out of which only the top slot is reinforced for the GPU. You also get three M.2 slots that accept drives up to 80mm long. NZXT attempted to get the most out of the B650 chipset, rivaling feature sets of more expensive boards.

Just like other premium motherboards in this collection, the NZXT N7 B650E also comes with a pre-installed I/O shield. You get ten USB ports in total, out of which four are USB 3.2 Gen2 ports, four are USB 3.2 Gen1 ports, and the final two are USB 2.0 ports. You also get an HDMI port along with BIOS flashback and clear CMOS buttons and a Wi-Fi antenna. The performance of the N7 B650E is on par with a lot of other premium motherboards on the market. It's a fantastic option for those looking at a clean motherboard that hides the PCB design. It's particularly good for builds involving NZXT PC cases or other NZXT components.

ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI
Best compact

Build a super-compact gaming PC with this board

$400 $470 Save $70

This compact ITX ASUS ROG Strix X670E motherboard has everything you'll need for a powerful PC that can fit inside a smaller form factor chassis.

Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
10+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
2x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
ALC4050
Networking
Intel 2.5Gb, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB 3.2-C Gen 2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 4.0, 5x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 3x USB-A 2.0
Pros
  • Compact design
  • Good performance
Cons
  • Limited connectivity
  • Expensive
$400 at Amazon $426 at Newegg

This ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi motherboard is one to buy if you fancy building a small form factor PC. It's seriously small, rocking just one PCI slot. This size does mean you will lose some features, including only having one PCI slot, but you're able to create something special with a smaller footprint. ASUS managed to include a 10+2 phase power delivery design and the flagship X670E chipset. This means you should be able to run even the AMD Ryzen 9 without issues, though overclocking may produce some unstable results with the higher-tier processors. The single PCIe slot we mentioned earlier is 5.0, so too is one of two available PCIe M.2 slots for SSDs.

There's not a lot to the board itself, due to the size, but ASUS did a fine job in making it aesthetically pleasing and stealthy enough to fit inside more themed builds. There are a few headers for cooling and RGB lighting integration, as well as all the usual power connections. An added bonus of the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi is the inclusion of the ROG Strix Hive. This external device hooks up to the motherboard and offers additional functionality, including an integrated DAC. This can prove useful if you position your compact PC somewhere that makes the rear I/O difficult to access.

For rear I/O, Asus managed to squeeze in two USB-C 4.0 ports, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, and a further three USB-A 2.0 ports, so even the most compact PC system will be able to handle a few gaming peripherals.

MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
Best for enthusiasts

Try your luck at the overclocking leaderboard!

$1200 $1262 Save $62

The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is an extreme motherboard with just about everything you could ask for in an enthusiastic platform. Designed for heavier use, MSI offers multiple reinforced PCIe 5.0 slots, four M.2 slots, robust power delivery with effective VRM cooling, and a striking dark board design.

Brand
MSI
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-5600
Form Factor
E-ATX
CPU Support
AMD Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
24+2+1
PCI Slots
3x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
4x M.2, 8x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC4082 Codec, ESS ES9280AQ
Networking
10GbE, 2.5GbE, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 4x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 7x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2
Pros
  • Impressive specifications
  • Excellent performance
  • Board design
Cons
  • Incredibly expensive
  • Overkill for most systems
$1200 at Amazon $1243 at Newegg

You likely won't require anything close to the MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE, but it's designed for those with more advanced hardware and plans for some overclocking. Let's start with the impressive power setup, consisting of a 24+2+1 phase delivery design. The VRMs are covered by a large heatsink and MSI made good use of some thermal pads and MOSFET passive cooling. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 codec with help from the ESS ES9280AQ, which means movies, music, and (most importantly) games will sound amazing when paired with the best gaming headset.

For RAM, MSI states the board will happily support DDR5 modules with speeds up to 5600MT/s. The DIMM slots are conveniently located next to the migrated 4-pin EPS sockets. In fact, just about every major connection header on this motherboard has been adjusted to face perpendicular to the motherboard, instead of outward. This should aid in cable routing, especially for the thicker cabling. All three PCI slots are reinforced and support PCIe 5.0 for maximum bandwidth. If all that wasn't enough, there are four M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0), and 10GbE networking.

The rear I/O is just as ridiculous with seven USB-A 3.2 Gen2 ports, and two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 ports. The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is incredibly expensive, almost triple the price of some of our recommendations here, but the price may be worth it if you require all these advanced features not typically found on lesser boards.

ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best workstation

For serious computing and performance

$880 $900 Save $20

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

Memory Slots
8
Memory Type
DDR4-4400
Form Factor
E-ATX
Wi-Fi
Yes
CPU Support
AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 3000/5000
Chipset
WRX80
Socket
sWRX
Power Phases
8
PCI Slots
7x PCIe 4.0
Storage
8x SATA, 2x M.2 SSD
Audio
Realtek ALC4050H+ALC1220
Networking
2x Marvell (Aquantia) 10Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB 2.0
Rear I/O
2x Thunderbolt 4, 4x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen1
Pros
  • Amazing performance
  • Incredible bandwidth
  • Dual 10GbE networking
Cons
  • Expensive
  • Supports only Threadripper CPUs
$900 at Newegg $880 at Amazon

Last, but not least, we've added the ASRock WRX80 Creator as our pick for the best motherboard for those looking to build a HEDT PC with an AMD Ryzen Threadripper processor. The ASRock WRX80 Creator is one of the most expensive motherboards you can buy, but that's because it comes with a long list of features that are not necessarily found on any of the mainstream motherboards we've listed above. It serves up to 64 lanes of PCIe at Gen4 speeds. It's also only the motherboard in this list that comes with a dual-fan heatsink covering its 8 105A voltage regulator phases.

The ASRock WRX80 Creator is an ATX motherboard that carries an AMD sWRX socket. Two 40mm fans are cooling the VRM, in addition to the regular 50mm fan that we usually see on other mainstream AMD motherboards. These VRM fans are enough to tame even the most powerful, fully-overclocked system without a hitch. There are two available M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots for SSDs, joining the seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots for expansion cards.

The I/O shield comes pre-installed, which isn't all that surprising considering this is a high-end premium motherboard. You won't see any RGB lighting, however. The ASRock WRX80 Creator comes with 8 DIMM slots for impressive system memory capacity. You get plenty of headers for fan/pump, RGB lights, USB, and more. Overclocking is also in cards since this is a high-performance motherboard that's designed to push even the most demanding HEDT CPUs on the market.

Choosing the best AMD motherboard

It's not required to spend a fortune to enjoy the best of what's offered by AMD desktop CPUs, and we believe our collection here provides ample choice, covering multiple generations. We view the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming as the best X670 chipset-based motherboard one can buy right now for a high-end PC build. It has a solid power phase delivery system for stable CPU overclocking, plenty of connectivity, and an excellent set of features ... for a price.

The ASUS TUF Gaming B650-PLUS is also a great option for those looking to buy an affordable AM5 motherboard. It has many of the same features offered by the X670E-E Gaming from ASUS but cuts a few to bring the price down. If you do not need the absolute best platform for enthusiast overclocking and likely won't saturate all the available PCIe lanes offered by the B650 chipset, this motherboard will be more than enough.

Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall
$460 $500 Save $40

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$460 at Amazon $460 at Newegg $460 at B&H

item.title: This Asus gaming laptop with a Ryzen 7 and 165Hz screen is now $430 off in this limited-time deal
item.itunes:title: This Asus gaming laptop with a Ryzen 7 and 165Hz screen is now $430 off in this limited-time deal
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
ASUS ROG Zephyrus G14
$1000 $1430 Save $430

The Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 is a powerful and compact laptop that's great for gaming and everyday use. While this laptop normally comes in at $1429.99, it's on sale right now with a deal that knocks $430 off the original price, bringing it down to $999.99.

$1000 at Best Buy

Gaming laptops are expensive. But if you're on the lookout, there's always a great chance that you can grab one on sale for a good price, saving you hundreds in the process. Of course, it's tough to spot the really good deals, so we've taken care of that part for you. Right now, you can score this Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 laptop for $430 less, bringing the price down to $999.99 for a limited time.

What's great about the Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 laptop?

Well, the most captivating thing about this laptop has to be its design. It comes in all white, and has a unique look that easily makes it stand out in a sea of laptops. The laptop features a 14-inch 2560x1440 resolution display with a refresh rate of 165Hz. In addition to the lightning quick refresh rate, you'll also get excellent colors and black levels, with up to 500 nits of brightness.

The device is powered by an AMD Ryzen 7-7735HS processor, 16GB RAM, and 512GB of internal M.2 SSD storage. Furthermore, the laptop also offers plenty of graphical power with its Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050. Despite its compact size, you also get excellent connectivity with two USB-A and two USB-C ports. You also get Wi-Fi 6E, a 720p webcam, RGB backlit keyboard, and more.

For the most part, this is a complete package when it comes to a gaming laptop that's thin, light, and also powerful. While it's not the cheapest price out there for a gaming laptop, it's one of the best values available for a current laptop. So if you've been looking to get yourself a new gaming laptop, be sure to grab this deal while you still can.

$1000 at Best Buy

Gaming laptops are expensive. But if you're on the lookout, there's always a great chance that you can grab one on sale for a good price, saving you hundreds in the process. Of course, it's tough to spot the really good deals, so we've taken care of that part for you. Right now, you can score this Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 laptop for $430 less, bringing the price down to $999.99 for a limited time.

What's great about the Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 laptop?

Well, the most captivating thing about this laptop has to be its design. It comes in all white, and has a unique look that easily makes it stand out in a sea of laptops. The laptop features a 14-inch 2560x1440 resolution display with a refresh rate of 165Hz. In addition to the lightning quick refresh rate, you'll also get excellent colors and black levels, with up to 500 nits of brightness.

The device is powered by an AMD Ryzen 7-7735HS processor, 16GB RAM, and 512GB of internal M.2 SSD storage. Furthermore, the laptop also offers plenty of graphical power with its Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050. Despite its compact size, you also get excellent connectivity with two USB-A and two USB-C ports. You also get Wi-Fi 6E, a 720p webcam, RGB backlit keyboard, and more.

For the most part, this is a complete package when it comes to a gaming laptop that's thin, light, and also powerful. While it's not the cheapest price out there for a gaming laptop, it's one of the best values available for a current laptop. So if you've been looking to get yourself a new gaming laptop, be sure to grab this deal while you still can.

item.title: How to install Windows 11 on almost any unsupported PC
item.itunes:title: How to install Windows 11 on almost any unsupported PC
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Check if TPM and Secure Boot are enabled
  • Using Rufus to create a custom ISO
  • Bypassing requirements with the Registry Editor
  • Install Windows 11 with launch parameters
  • Conclusion

Windows 11 is the latest version of the most popular operating system in the world, and it's been available for ove two years now... as long as you have a supported computer. This version of Windows changed a lot, from the design language to dropping support for a lot of older hardware. Indeed, Windows 11 has some hefty system requirements compared to previous versions of Windows, so there are a lot of unsupported PCs you can't install it on.

The most notable change for many is the new requirement for a Trusted Platform Module (TPM), but it also bumps the minimum RAM required to 4GB and it requires an x64-based processor, on top of having a fairly restrictive list of supported CPUs.

All of that means that many users are left without an official path to upgrade to Windows 11, even if their PC still runs well enough for them. While there isn't much you can do about compatibility if you have an x86 CPU, you can bypass the requirements for specific CPUs or TPM support, which allows most Windows PCs to upgrade to Windows 11. In this article, we're going to show you how to do that.

Check if TPM and Secure Boot are enabled

One thing that's worth keeping in mind before starting this process is that in some cases, your PC may have TPM or Secure Boot support (another Windows 11 requirement), but it's disabled in the BIOS, so first, you should make sure Secure Boot is enabled alongside TPM. Here's how:

  1. Restart your computer.
  2. When you see the first signs of life, such as the manufacturer's logo, repeatedly press Esc, F1, F2, or F12. Different brands use different keys, and you can learn more in our guide to enter the BIOS.
  3. Once in the BIOS settings, look for settings called Secure Boot and TPM (this may also be labeled as Security Device or have other text around it). They'll usually be under a security section, but it may vary depending on your computer. Some PCs may have a firmware TPM (fTPM) rather than a hardware TPM. Either option should work.
  4. Make sure both are enabled.
  5. Save your changes and exit.

After this, you may be able to install Windows 11 on your PC through the usual methods. If you don't find these settings, they might not be supported, which is where this guide comes in.

Bear in mind that if you install Windows 11 on an unsupported PC, you may not be able to get future updates. Security updates shouldn't be much of a problem, but you may end up needing to repeat this process if Microsoft releases a big update, like Windows 11 version 22H2 was. Additionally, some unsupported hardware has a good reason to not be supported. Performance and stability may not be the best on some older PCs.

Using Rufus to create a custom ISO

Rufus is a very useful tool for creating bootable installation drives for Windows and other operating systems. It's been around for a long time, and with Windows 11, the developer actually added some features to make it easier to install the new OS on unsupported machines. For this, you'll need a USB flash drive with nothing on it (or data you don't care about losing), but otherwise, this is easily the best method for anyone to use. If the USB drive has important data, be sure to create a backup before you start.

Here's what you need to do to get this working:

  1. Start by downloading an ISO file for Windows 11 on this page. The option to download an ISO is the third one, but you can also create an ISO using the Media Creation Tool on this page.
  2. Download the latest version of Rufus from the official website. It comes in both installable and portable versions, and you can use whatever you prefer.
  3. Plug in the USB flash drive you're going to use as installation media.

    We'd recommend unplugging any other drives so you don't risk erasing them by accident.

  4. Launch Rufus, and pay attention to the options you'll see here. Start by choosing the right flash drive under Device near the top.
  5. Under Boot selection click the Select button and look for the ISO file you downloaded. A few default settings will be applied, and you shouldn't need to change them. You can change the name of the drive if you want it to be easier to identify, though.
  6. Click Start at the bottom of the window.
  7. You'll see a prompt with a series of options, but the only one you need is at the top. This will disable all the compatibility checks for Secure Boot, TPM, specific CPUs, and even RAM (though we still wouldn't recommend installing Windows 11 if you don't meet the RAM requirements). It should be enabled by default, but if it isn't make sure to enable it.
  8. Click OK and Rufus will start moving the Windows 11 installation files to the flash drive. This process can take a while, especially if you have a slower USB port.
  9. When the process is finished, you'll have a USB flash drive that you can use to install Windows 11 on almost any PC that doesn't meet the requirements. Open the USB drive and launch the setup file to begin the installation.

    Alternatively, you can use the USB drive as a boot device if you want to perform a clean install. For that, check out our guide on how to install Windows 11 where we explore that specific path.

  10. Follow the installation process as normal. Click Accept when you see the Windows 11 license terms, and then Install when you see the summary screen for the installation options. Windows 11 will begin installing.

Once it finishes installing, you may see some additional configuration steps, but after that, you'll be on the Windows 11 desktop. You're ready to use Windows 11 as you would on an officially supported PC. But you'll have to keep in mind that some of these requirements exist for a reason, so some older hardware may experience poor performance or stability.

Bypassing requirements with the Registry Editor

One way you can try to bypass the system requirements for Windows 11 is to modify some registry values on your PC using the Registry Editor. Using the Registry Editor isn't something we recommend inexperienced users do, but if you know how to get around and you follow the steps below, you should be able to do this without much of a problem.

This method is ideal if you have an older TPM (such as a TPM 1.2) or if the only problem you're facing is having a supported CPU. It's not going to work for PCs with no TPM at all or to bypass other requirements.

Either way, the first thing you'll want to do is download an ISO for Windows 11, just like we did for the method above. Then follow these steps:

  1. Open the Start menu and type regedit, then choose the first search result.
  2. The Registry Editor should open. On the left-side pane, navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\Setup\MoSetup.
  3. Right-click the empty space on the right and select New, then DWORD (32-bit) Value. Name it AllowUpgradesWithUnsupportedTPMOrCPU.
  4. Double-click it and set the value to 1, then click OK.
  5. Now, double-click the ISO file you downloaded to mount it as a virtual drive.
  6. The drive should automatically open after mounting it, or you can find it on the This PC page. Run the setup file inside and begin the installation.
  7. If your only hurdle was CPU compatibility or having an older TPM, the installation should now proceed as normal.

If that still doesn't work, it may be because your PC has no TPM whatsoever, which may be the case for many custom-built PCs, for example.

Install Windows 11 with launch parameters

There's one last trick you can try if you don't have a USB drive and you're running into other issues. This one can be a bit risky for an upgrade due to potential compatibility and driver issues, however. We would definitely steer less experienced users toward the Rufus solution at the top.

This trick involves running the Windows 11 installer as if it were a Windows Server installer, which bypasses the compatibility checks that would usually prevent you from installing Windows 11. It can cause compatibility issues with some drivers and other settings if you're upgrading an existing PC, but there are ways to make it safer. This method was first shared by AveYo on GitHub. Here is what you need to do:

  1. Download a Windows 11 ISO, just as we did for the methods above.
  2. Double-click and mount the ISO as a virtual drive, and the new drive should open automatically. Take note of the drive letter in the address bar. In our case, it's E:, but it may vary.
  3. Open a Command Prompt window. You can do this by searching for cmd in the Start menu and pressing Enter.
  4. Enter the following text, replacing E: with the correct drive letter for your specific case. 
    E:\setup.exe /Product Server /Compat IgnoreWarning /MigrateDrivers All
  5. The setup should launch, but labeled as a Windows Server installer.
  6. Click through the process as you normally would, and Windows 11 will begin installing.

If you followed all of these steps correctly, you should soon have your computer running Windows 11 without any problems.

Conclusion

There are many reasons why Windows 11 may not run on your specific PC, but this workaround with Rufus is currently the best way to fix all of them. There are some things you can't work around, but most of the big new requirements for Windows 11 can be bypassed this way. It does require a USB drive, which not everyone will have, but it's worth it for the ease of use and the low level of risk. If you really don't want to get a USB drive, the other solutions should also help you.

Of course, the best way to get Windows 11 is probably to buy a new PC that supports it. We have a list of PCs that support Windows 11, but any Windows laptop you can buy today should ship with Windows 11, or at least support it. You can always check out our round-up of the best laptops available right now if you're looking for recommendations.

item.title: Best Steam Deck docks in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Steam Deck docks in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
If you've finally picked up a Steam Deck, and want to take your portable gaming to the next level, then you might want to consider buying a dock. Docks not only provide you with a great way to charge your Steam Deck but also give you a few useful ports to connect to an external display or use wired peripherals like a keyboard and mouse. If you've got one all-new Steam Deck OLED, and are wondering if it needs a specific dock, rest assured, as it's compatible with all the docks we've covered in this list. We've scoured the length and breadth of Steam Deck dock options out there and picked the very best in case you aren't sure which one to get!

  • Official Steam Deck Dock
    Official option
    $89 at Steam
  • Source: JSAUX

  • YYDSTDK Steam Deck Dock

item.content:encoded:
If you've finally picked up a Steam Deck, and want to take your portable gaming to the next level, then you might want to consider buying a dock. Docks not only provide you with a great way to charge your Steam Deck but also give you a few useful ports to connect to an external display or use wired peripherals like a keyboard and mouse. If you've got one all-new Steam Deck OLED, and are wondering if it needs a specific dock, rest assured, as it's compatible with all the docks we've covered in this list. We've scoured the length and breadth of Steam Deck dock options out there and picked the very best in case you aren't sure which one to get!
